turns.content: What brings you in today?

Hi there. For the past three weeks, I’ve had this lingering pain in my right shoulder after I tweaked it during a headstand in class. I initially thought it would resolve with rest, but it’s still limiting my range of motion—especially when I try to weight-bear or lift my arm overhead. As a yoga instructor, I can’t demonstrate key poses like
When did this start?

It started abruptly during a class about three weeks ago — I distinctly remember it being a Tuesday morning. I was transitioning into a headstand and felt a sharp, pinching sensation right in the front of my shoulder as I pushed up. I stopped immediately, but the ache never fully went away.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

The pain is localized right at the front of my shoulder, sort of along the joint line — it feels like a dull ache most of the time, but it becomes sharper if I try to lift my arm above shoulder height or push through my hands in poses like downward dog. There's also a bit of tenderness if I press into the area, and I notice it's worse when I reach behind my back, like trying to grab my bra strap.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, not at all. I've had no fever, chills, or any signs of illness. The discomfort has been completely localized to the shoulder itself, with no swelling or redness that I can see.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not on any prescription medications regularly. For the first week after the injury, I tried over-the-counter ibuprofen — 400 mg maybe two or three times a day — but it only took the edge off without really addressing the problem, so I stopped after a few days. I've just been using ice and gentle range-of-motion exercises since then.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?

No, I don't have any chronic medical conditions. I'm generally very healthy and active — I teach yoga six days a week and practice daily, so this is really unusual for me. I've never had any shoulder issues or injuries before this.
Are you allergic to any medications?

No, I'm not allergic to any medications that I'm aware of. I've never had a reaction to anything I've taken, including the ibuprofen I tried recently — just the usual mild stomach upset if I took it on an empty stomach.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Yes, I've been diligent about rest — I completely stopped doing inversions or any weight-bearing through my arms, and I've been icing the area for about 15 minutes a couple of times a day. I also tried some very gentle pendulum swings and doorframe stretches, but anything that loads the joint seems to aggravate the pain rather than help it. I haven't tried heat or anything like a brace.
Does anything make it better or worse?

What really aggravates it is any weight-bearing through that arm — downward dog, plank, even just pushing myself up from lying down. Raising my arm above shoulder level or reaching behind my back, like to fasten a bra, triggers that sharp pinch every time. What helps is keeping my arm resting by my side or supported on a cushion — the dull ache seems to settle when I'm not using it at all.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been quite disruptive, honestly. I've had to modify almost every class I teach — I can demonstrate verbally but I can't join in on the poses that require arm support, which feels awkward and limits my connection with students. Even simple things like carrying my yoga mat or reaching for a cup in an overhead cabinet make me wince. I've also stopped my own personal practice entirely for the past three weeks, which is frustrating because that's usually how I decompress.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?

I don't smoke at all. I do have a glass of wine maybe once a week with dinner, but nothing beyond that — and I haven't had any alcohol since the injury, just in case it might interfere with healing or increase inflammation. I'm pretty careful about what I put in my body given my profession and lifestyle.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

Not that I know of. Both my parents are fairly active and haven't had any shoulder issues — my dad had some lower back trouble when he was younger, but nothing with the joints. So as far as I'm aware, there's no family history of recurrent dislocations or rotator cuff problems.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

No, this is completely new to me. I've never had any shoulder pain or injury in the past — not even a minor strain. That's part of why I'm a bit concerned; it's been three weeks and it's not resolving on its own like I expected.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?

Yes, that makes perfect sense. I appreciate you walking through all of this with me — it helps to feel like I’m being thorough rather than just rushing to a conclusion. I do have a question, though: based on what I’ve described, how likely is it that this is just a strain versus something more structural, like a labral or rotator cuff issue? I’m just trying to understand what might be going on under the surface.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, I'd say it's around a 3 — it's not excruciating, but it's that persistent, nagging ache that reminds me every time I try to move my arm in a certain way. At its worst, when I accidentally put weight on it or reach
Do you have any questions for me?

Actually, yes — I do have a few. Given that it's been three weeks and I'm still having trouble with weight-bearing and overhead motion, what's a realistic timeline for recovery if it's something like a strain versus a tear? And in the meantime, are there specific movements or positions I should absolutely avoid, besides the obvious inversions and planks? I'd love to keep teaching safely without making things worse.
What do you think might be going on?

From what I've read and from my own anatomy background, I'm wondering if it might be a mild rotator cuff strain or possibly something involving the labrum — especially since the pain is so specific to the front of the shoulder and worsens with weight-bearing and overhead movements. I know that a headstand puts a lot of compressive force through the shoulder joint,
Is there anything else you think I should know?

I think that covers most of it. One more thing — I have noticed a bit of a clicking sensation when I rotate my arm in certain directions, especially when I'm lying down and trying to test my range of motion. It's not painful exactly, just an audible pop, but it makes me wonder if something inside the joint is moving out of place. Does that sound like it fits with what you were thinking?
